seoTitle: How to Connect Facebook Pixel to Your Wix Website
metaDescription: Learn how to add your Meta Pixel ID to Wix's Marketing Integrations panel to track conversions and optimize ad campaigns.
intro: Connecting a Facebook (Meta) Pixel to your Wix site lets you track visitor behavior and conversion events for your ad campaigns without any custom code. This guide covers finding your Pixel ID, entering it into Wix's native Marketing Integrations panel, and verifying the tracking connection is live.

steps.heading: Open Marketing & SEO
steps.body: From your Wix dashboard, navigate to the Marketing & SEO hub.

steps.heading: Go to Marketing Integrations
steps.body: Open the Marketing Integrations preference panel.

steps.heading: Find Meta Pixel
steps.body: Locate the Meta Pixel (Facebook Pixel) connection option and click Connect.

steps.heading: Enter your Pixel ID
steps.body: Input your unique 15-digit Meta Pixel ID into the configuration field.

steps.heading: Configure event tracking
steps.body: Set up advanced matching parameters if you want more granular tracking of events like add-to-cart or checkout.

steps.heading: Verify the connection
steps.body: Visit your live domain and use browser developer tools or Meta's Pixel Helper extension to confirm data is transmitting.

faq.question: Where do I find my Meta Pixel ID?
faq.answer: Your unique 15-digit Pixel ID is available in Meta Business Suite under your dataset or pixel settings.

faq.question: Do I need to write any code to connect the pixel?
faq.answer: No, Wix's native Marketing Integrations panel lets you enter the Pixel ID directly without custom code.

faq.question: Can I track specific events like add-to-cart automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once connected, standard e-commerce events like page views and checkout milestones can sync automatically depending on your setup.

faq.question: How do I confirm the pixel is actually firing?
faq.answer: Use a browser extension like Meta's Pixel Helper or your browser's developer tools to audit whether tracking events are firing correctly.
